title: stephanomeria exigua subsp. carotifera
intro: Stephanomeria exigua subsp. carotifera, also known as a desert heliotrope, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a member of the sunflower family and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant has been traditionally used to promote overall health and well-being, with reported benefits including reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and enhanced immune function. Desert heliotrope can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in conjunction with other medicinal plants to support holistic health.
